summary_en: Kaga Electronics (TSE Prime-listed) to acquire all shares of Shinko Shoji via tender offer at ¥1,580/share for 30 business days (May 18–Jun 26, 2026). Minimum acceptance threshold 19.23M shares (66.67% post-acquisition stake). Intent: complete subsidiary status and delisting. Shinko board recommends acceptance; shareholders decide final participation.
title_en: Notice Regarding Expression of Opinion on Public Tender Offer for Shinko Shoji Shares by Kaga Electronics
translation_en_brief: On May 15, 2026, Shinko Shoji (ticker 8141, TSE Prime) announced that its board of directors has expressed a recommendation in favor of a public tender offer initiated by Kaga Electronics Co., Ltd., a listed electronic components distributor. The offer runs for 30 business days from May 18 to June 26, 2026, at a fixed price of ¥1,580 per common share. The minimum acceptance threshold is 19,226,700 shares, which would result in Kaga Electronics holding 66.67% of Shinko's voting shares post-acquisition. The stated intent is to make Shinko a wholly owned subsidiary and subsequently delist it from the TSE Prime market. The board approved the recommendation after determining that Kaga Electronics currently owns 515,000 shares (1.74% stake) and has existing commercial relationships with Shinko in semiconductor and electronic components trading. The board resolution was approved unanimously by all directors without conflicts of interest, including audit committee members. Shinko's shareholders retain discretion on whether to tender their shares.
